title: Mission
body:
Mission
Create the definitive career guide for AEM developers.

  • Evidence over assumptions
  • Long-term value
  • Publication quality
  • Continuous improvement

title: Career Strategy
summary: Long-term direction for AEM developers in India.
body: In India, AEM roles show up across product companies, GCCs, and agencies / SIs — each has different pace, ownership, and interview style. Use Target Companies to build a list; use How to Apply for the workflow. Prefer depth in AEM (Sites, Cloud Service, Dispatcher, integrations) over shallow “full-stack everything.” Stay interview-ready even when you are not switching: keep LinkedIn/Naukri current, do a calm market-check interview every few months, and close gaps interviews expose. This is personal research — not a ranking of employers; every organisation has pros and cons.

title: Professional Branding
summary: Positioning and reusable messaging for AEM roles.
body: Present yourself as an AEM Developer with clear skills in Sites, Cloud Service, Dispatcher, and integrations. Fill placeholders with your real metrics and projects — never invent impact. Use the templates below for LinkedIn, Naukri, outreach, and resume bullets.

title: Core Skills
body: Build from platform basics (Sites, Sling, HTL, OSGi, Dispatcher, Java for AEM) into Cloud Service, headless, and Experience Platform integrations. The table below includes official Adobe / Apache docs where available — use them as the source of truth, not random blog posts.

title: Learning Roadmap
body: Start here if you are new or rusty: AEM foundation (architecture → Sling/HTL → Dispatcher). Then: Cloud Service & Edge. When you need personalization / data: Experience Platform integration. Each step lists curated official Adobe / Apache docs — go at your own pace.

title: Target Companies
summary: AEM employers for your target list.

title: How to Apply
summary: Step-by-step apply workflow for AEM roles — resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, outreach, and tools.
body: This guide is for staying job-ready, not only active job search. Practical steps for Indian job portals and notice periods — plus learning from every interview. Templates are in Professional Branding.

title: Community
summary: Independent LinkedIn groups for AEM developers (discovery only).
body: Peer LinkedIn groups for openings, tips, and questions. These are independent communities — not run by this playbook, Adobe, or listed employers. Follow each group’s rules; be polite and don’t spam resumes. Combine group leads with Target Companies and your own LinkedIn/Naukri search.

title: Career Command Center
body:
• Master Company Database ✓
  • Recruiter CRM (planned)
  • Application Tracker (planned)
  • Interview Tracker (planned)
  • Learning Tracker (roadmaps + glossary live)
  • Decision Log

title: Glossary
summary: AEM and DXP terms used throughout this playbook.
body: Short definitions of AEM, DXP, and related terms used in interviews and roadmaps. Prefer Adobe Experience League and Apache docs when you need full detail.

title: Interview Prep
summary: Technical and behavioural questions for AEM roles.
body: Interview outcomes depend on your knowledge, how the interviewer runs the loop, what is on your resume and projects, and fundamentals (JCR, Java, Sling, OSGi, Dispatcher, HTL, ResourceResolver). Guidance below is a coach checklist — adapt it to your experience; it is not a script and not a guarantee of exact questions. Honest AEM project work (without relying only on AI-generated answers) is the strongest prep; still study the basics so you can explain what you built.

title: About this data
summary: How to read this playbook — what is researched, what to confirm yourself.
body: This playbook is personal research shared to help AEM developers. Use it as a starting point — always confirm openings on employer careers pages and job boards.

title: Living Roadmap
body: Official Adobe documentation and courses referenced across this playbook (also linked from Learning Roadmap and Core Skills in product mode).
